item.title: [8] Tian-Ye-Jin scaling analysis of the anomalous Hall effect and strong electron-magnon scattering in the van der Waals ferromagnet Fe3GaTe2
item.description:
Last authors: Jun Hu, Baomin Wang

Authors: Lanfeng Liu, Yitao Zheng, Hongyuan Wang, et al.

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Anomalous Hall and vdW magnetism on a high-temperature ferromagnetic material.

Tags: #AHE #vdW #ferromagnet

Abstract: Author(s): Lanfeng Liu, Yitao Zheng, Hongyuan Wang, Bangyong Yan, Mengting Zou, Wei Li, Jun Hu, and Baomin Wang The significance of anomalous Hall effect (AHE) lies in its capability of the interconversion between spin and charge information. The van der Waals (vdW) ferromagnet $\mathrm{F}{\mathrm{e}}_{3}\mathrm{GaT}{\mathrm{e}}_{2}$ has attracted extensive interest owing to its high Curie temperature and str… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 184432] Published Mon May 11, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Lanfeng Liu, Yitao Zheng, Hongyuan Wang, Bangyong Yan, Mengting Zou, Wei Li, Jun Hu, and Baomin Wang

The significance of anomalous Hall effect (AHE) lies in its capability of the interconversion between spin and charge information. The van der Waals (vdW) ferromagnet Fe3GaTe2 has attracted extensive interest owing to its high Curie temperature and strong perpendicular magnetic anisotropy. Here, we …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 184432] Published Mon May 11, 2026
item.dc:title: [8] Tian-Ye-Jin scaling analysis of the anomalous Hall effect and strong electron-magnon scattering in the van der Waals ferromagnet Fe3GaTe2
item.dc:creator: Lanfeng Liu, Yitao Zheng, Hongyuan Wang, et al.
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Magnetism

item.title: [8] Spin-valley splitting in altermagnet/ferromagnet Ca(CoN)2/CoX (X=Cl2,Se) heterostructures
item.description:
Last authors: Chenqiang Hua, Fei Song

Authors: Zhaofeng Liang, Jinsen Zhang, Yao Wang, Chenqiang Hua, Fei Song

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Altermagnetism and heterostructures are key interests for this user.

Tags: #altermagnet #heterostructure #theory

Abstract: Author(s): Zhaofeng Liang, Jinsen Zhang, Yao Wang, Chenqiang Hua, and Fei Song Altermagnets with spin-layer coupling (SLC) enable symmetry-protected spin splitting without net magnetization. In the SLC altermagnet $\mathrm{Ca}{(\mathrm{CoN})}_{2}$ monolayer, which exhibits distinct sublayer-dependent spin and valley characteristics, we demonstrate that breaking the sublayer-ex… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 184434] Published Mon May 11, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Zhaofeng Liang, Jinsen Zhang, Yao Wang, Chenqiang Hua, and Fei Song

Altermagnets with spin-layer coupling (SLC) enable symmetry-protected spin splitting without net magnetization. In the SLC altermagnet Ca(CoN)2 monolayer, which exhibits distinct sublayer-dependent spin and valley characteristics, we demonstrate that breaking the sublayer-exchange symmetry S via fer…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 184434] Published Mon May 11, 2026
item.dc:title: [8] Spin-valley splitting in altermagnet/ferromagnet Ca(CoN)2/CoX (X=Cl2,Se) heterostructures
item.dc:creator: Zhaofeng Liang, Jinsen Zhang, Yao Wang, Chenqiang Hua, Fei Song

item.title: [7] Dynamics and thermodynamics of the S=52 almost Heisenberg triangular lattice antiferromagnet K2Mn(SeO3)2
item.description:
Last authors: Z. Yan, S. Gvasaliya, A. L. Chernyshev, and A. Zheludev

Authors: Mengze Zhu, V. Romerio, D. Moser, K. Yu. Povarov, R. Sibille, R. Wawrzyńczak, Z. Yan, S. Gvasaliya, A. L. Chernyshev, and A. Zheludev

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Neutron scattering study of a frustrated triangular lattice antiferromagnet.

Tags: #neutron-scattering #magnetism #frustration

Abstract: Author(s): Mengze Zhu, V. Romerio, D. Moser, K. Yu. Povarov, R. Sibille, R. Wawrzyńczak, Z. Yan, S. Gvasaliya, A. L. Chernyshev, and A. Zheludev We report calorimetric, magnetic, and neutron scattering studies on an $S=5/2$, nearly Heisenberg triangular-lattice antiferromagnet ${\mathrm{K}}_{2}\mathrm{Mn}{({\mathrm{SeO}}_{3})}_{2}$ with weak XXZ easy-axis anisotropy. Multiple magnetic phases are identified, including a noncollinear Y phase i… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 184436] Published Mon May 11, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Mengze Zhu, V. Romerio, D. Moser, K. Yu. Povarov, R. Sibille, R. Wawrzyńczak, Z. Yan, S. Gvasaliya, A. L. Chernyshev, and A. Zheludev

We report calorimetric, magnetic, and neutron scattering studies on an S=5/2, nearly Heisenberg triangular-lattice antiferromagnet K2Mn(SeO3)2 with weak XXZ easy-axis anisotropy. Multiple magnetic phases are identified, including a noncollinear Y phase in zero field, a field-induced collinear m=1/3 …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 184436] Published Mon May 11, 2026
item.dc:title: [7] Dynamics and thermodynamics of the S=52 almost Heisenberg triangular lattice antiferromagnet K2Mn(SeO3)2
item.dc:creator: Mengze Zhu, V. Romerio, D. Moser, K. Yu. Povarov, R. Sibille, R. Wawrzyńczak, Z. Yan, S. Gvasaliya, A. L. Chernyshev, and A. Zheludev

item.title: [7] Contrasting g-factor anisotropy in easy-plane and easy-axis van der Waals CrX3 (X=Cl, Br, I) magnetic materials
item.description:
Last authors: Yunhui Li, Yizheng Wu

Authors: Nan Jiang, Hongyue Xu, Haoran Chen, et al.

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Magnetic resonance and anisotropy in archetypal vdW magnets.

Tags: #vdW #magnetism

Abstract: Author(s): Nan Jiang, Hongyue Xu, Haoran Chen, Yuanfei Fan, Tong Wu, Zhen Cheng, Yizi Feng, Yunhui Li, and Yizheng Wu We systematically investigate $g$-factor anisotropy in two-dimensional van der Waals magnets $\mathrm{Cr}{X}_{3}$ $(X=\mathrm{Cl}, \mathrm{Br}, \mathrm{I})$ using broadband magnetic resonance for magnetic ordered states below the critical temperature $({T}_{\text{cr}})$ and for paramagnetic states a… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 184437] Published Mon May 11, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Nan Jiang, Hongyue Xu, Haoran Chen, Yuanfei Fan, Tong Wu, Zhen Cheng, Yizi Feng, Yunhui Li, and Yizheng Wu

We systematically investigate g-factor anisotropy in two-dimensional van der Waals magnets CrX3 (X=Cl, Br, I) using broadband magnetic resonance for magnetic ordered states below the critical temperature (Tcr) and for paramagnetic states above Tcr, with varied field orientations. Despite sharing the…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 184437] Published Mon May 11, 2026
item.dc:title: [7] Contrasting g-factor anisotropy in easy-plane and easy-axis van der Waals CrX3 (X=Cl, Br, I) magnetic materials
item.dc:creator: Nan Jiang, Hongyue Xu, Haoran Chen, et al.
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Magnetism

item.title: [9] Double-Q chiral stripe order in the anomalous Hall antiferromagnet CoNb3S6
item.description:
Last authors: Cristian D. Batista, Kemp W. Plumb

Authors: Ben Zager, Shang-Shun Zhang, Hana Schiff, et al.

Score 9/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Strong hit: anomalous Hall, circular dichroism scattering, and magnetic topology.; demoted from A: broad CM, not direct user/project hit

Tags: #AHE #circular-dichroism #REXS #magnetism

Abstract: Author(s): Ben Zager, Shang-Shun Zhang, Hana Schiff, Raymond Fan, Paul Steadman, Cristian D. Batista, and Kemp W. Plumb We present fine momentum space resolution resonant elastic x-ray scattering measurements of the magnetic structure of the metallic antiferromagnet ${\mathrm{CoNb}}_{3}{\mathrm{S}}_{6}$. Using circular dichroism and full linear polarization analysis of the magnetic scattering, we reveal a noncoplanar… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 184441] Published Mon May 11, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Ben Zager, Shang-Shun Zhang, Hana Schiff, Raymond Fan, Paul Steadman, Cristian D. Batista, and Kemp W. Plumb

We present fine momentum space resolution resonant elastic x-ray scattering measurements of the magnetic structure of the metallic antiferromagnet CoNb3S6. Using circular dichroism and full linear polarization analysis of the magnetic scattering, we reveal a noncoplanar double-Q (2Q) order that is c…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 184441] Published Mon May 11, 2026
item.dc:title: [9] Double-Q chiral stripe order in the anomalous Hall antiferromagnet CoNb3S6
item.dc:creator: Ben Zager, Shang-Shun Zhang, Hana Schiff, et al.

item.title: [7] Carrier-density dependence of magnetotransport in the correlated Dirac semimetal CaIrO3
item.description:
Last authors: Daisuke Hashizume, Yoshinori Tokura

Authors: Rinsuke Yamada, Jun Fujioka, Minoru Kawamura, et al.

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Experimental study on correlated Dirac semimetal relevant to topological materials interest.

Tags: #Dirac #correlated

Abstract: Author(s): Rinsuke Yamada, Jun Fujioka, Minoru Kawamura, Tatsuya Okawa, Yoshio Kaneko, Shiro Sakai, Motoaki Hirayama, Ryotaro Arita, Kiyohiro Adachi, Daisuke Hashizume, and Yoshinori Tokura We report the carrier-density dependence of the magnetotransport property in the correlated Dirac semimetal ${\mathrm{CaIrO}}_{3}$. In the dilute carrier density region (${n}_{\mathrm{H}} ∼2.2×{10}^{16}\phantom{\rule{0.16em}{0ex}}{\mathrm{cm}}^{−3}$) at $2\phantom{\rule{0.16em}{0ex}}\mathrm{K}$, the… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 205126] Published Mon May 11, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Rinsuke Yamada, Jun Fujioka, Minoru Kawamura, Tatsuya Okawa, Yoshio Kaneko, Shiro Sakai, Motoaki Hirayama, Ryotaro Arita, Kiyohiro Adachi, Daisuke Hashizume, and Yoshinori Tokura

We report the carrier-density dependence of the magnetotransport property in the correlated Dirac semimetal CaIrO3. In the dilute carrier density region (nH ∼2.2×1016cm−3) at 2K, the mobility exceeds 1.0×105cm2/Vs at 2K, and the transverse magnetoresistance reaches 2000% at 12T. The analysis of quan…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 205126] Published Mon May 11, 2026
item.dc:title: [7] Carrier-density dependence of magnetotransport in the correlated Dirac semimetal CaIrO3
item.dc:creator: Rinsuke Yamada, Jun Fujioka, Minoru Kawamura, et al.
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited

item.title: [7] Magnetically tunable quantum Hall triple-antidot molecules realized in suspended graphene
item.description:
Last authors: Dmitri V. Averin, Xu Du

Authors: Naomi Mizuno, Dmitri V. Averin, Xu Du

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Experimental study on graphene quantum Hall states.

Tags: #graphene #quantumHall

Abstract: Author(s): Naomi Mizuno, Dmitri V. Averin, and Xu Du We report the realization and modeling of a triple-antidot molecule hosting three interacting quantum Hall quasiparticles, with tunnel coupling between antidots tunable via the magnetic field. The measured tunneling conductance spectrum reveals the molecular energy levels arising from the interantid… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 205128] Published Mon May 11, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Naomi Mizuno, Dmitri V. Averin, and Xu Du

We report the realization and modeling of a triple-antidot molecule hosting three interacting quantum Hall quasiparticles, with tunnel coupling between antidots tunable via the magnetic field. The measured tunneling conductance spectrum reveals the molecular energy levels arising from the interantid…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 205128] Published Mon May 11, 2026

item.title: [7] Tunable directional propagation of spin plasmons in strained two-dimensional d-wave altermagnets

Last authors: Kehan Liu, Mingwen Zhao

Authors: Haotian Sun, Yueheng Du, Zhihua Zhang, Kehan Liu, Mingwen Zhao

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Theoretical study of altermagnets, a high-priority topic for this user.

Tags: #altermagnet #monolayer

Abstract: Author(s): Haotian Sun, Yueheng Du, Zhihua Zhang, Kehan Liu, and Mingwen Zhao The emergence of altermagnets offers a promising platform for studying spin-charge coupling scenarios. In this study, we investigate the significant regulatory influence of uniaxial strain on the electronic and plasmonic properties of two-dimensional $d$-wave altermagnets, specifically, monolayer ${… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 205413] Published Mon May 11, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Haotian Sun, Yueheng Du, Zhihua Zhang, Kehan Liu, and Mingwen Zhao

The emergence of altermagnets offers a promising platform for studying spin-charge coupling scenarios. In this study, we investigate the significant regulatory influence of uniaxial strain on the electronic and plasmonic properties of two-dimensional d-wave altermagnets, specifically, monolayer V2Se…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 205413] Published Mon May 11, 2026

item.title: [7] Optical signatures of flat bands and anisotropic quantum geometry in magic-angle twisted bilayer graphene
item.description:
Last authors: Pok Man Chiu

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Optical conductivity signatures for flat bands in tBLG.

Tags: #graphene #twistedbilayer #flatband

Abstract: Author(s): Pok Man Chiu We study the degree of band flatness and anisotropic quantum geometry in magic-angle twisted bilayer graphene by varying the twist angle and the lattice relaxation through optical conductivity. We show that the degree of band flatness and its quantum geometry can be revealed through optical absorpti… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 205416] Published Mon May 11, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Pok Man Chiu

We study the degree of band flatness and anisotropic quantum geometry in magic-angle twisted bilayer graphene by varying the twist angle and the lattice relaxation through optical conductivity. We show that the degree of band flatness and its quantum geometry can be revealed through optical absorpti…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 205416] Published Mon May 11, 2026

item.title: [7] Topological magnon-plasmon hybrids
item.description:
Last authors: Pieter M. Gunnink, Alexander Mook

Authors: Tomoki Hirosawa, Pieter M. Gunnink, Alexander Mook

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Topological hybrids in vdW materials, relevant to magnetic topological materials interest.

Tags: #vanderWaals #topological #magnon

Abstract: Author(s): Tomoki Hirosawa, Pieter M. Gunnink, and Alexander Mook Magnons and plasmons are collective excitations of spins and electric charges, respectively. In two-dimensional van der Waals layers, they can be hybridized via magnetic dipole coupling. Here, the authors report the theoretical discovery of topological magnon-plasmon hybrids. Within this two-dimensional system, they demonstrate the emergence of intrinsic anomalous thermal Hall and spin-Nernst effects as well as chiral magnon-plasmon edge states, suggesting topological magnon-plasmonics as a new field at the intersection of magnonics, plasmonics, and topology. [Phys. Rev. B 113, L180404] Published Mon May 11, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Tomoki Hirosawa, Pieter M. Gunnink, and Alexander Mook

Magnons and plasmons are collective excitations of spins and electric charges, respectively. In two-dimensional van der Waals layers, they can be hybridized via magnetic dipole coupling. Here, the authors report the theoretical discovery of topological magnon-plasmon hybrids. Within this two-dimensional system, they demonstrate the emergence of intrinsic anomalous thermal Hall and spin-Nernst effects as well as chiral magnon-plasmon edge states, suggesting topological magnon-plasmonics as a new field at the intersection of magnonics, plasmonics, and topology.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L180404] Published Mon May 11, 2026
item.dc:title: [7] Topological magnon-plasmon hybrids
item.dc:creator: Tomoki Hirosawa, Pieter M. Gunnink, Alexander Mook
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Magnetism

item.title: [7] Superconducting dome in ionic liquid gated homoepitaxial strontium titanate thin films
item.description:
Last authors: David Goldhaber-Gordon, Evgeny Mikheev

Authors: Sushant Padhye, Jin Yue, Shivasheesh Varshney, Bharat Jalan, David Goldhaber-Gordon, Evgeny Mikheev

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Experimental study of unconventional superconductivity in oxide thin films.

Tags: #superconduct

Abstract: Author(s): Sushant Padhye, Jin Yue, Shivasheesh Varshney, Bharat Jalan, David Goldhaber-Gordon, and Evgeny Mikheev In this work, we patterned a two-dimensional electron gas (2DEG) on the surface of a ${\mathrm{SrTiO}}_{3}$ thin film grown homoepitaxially on ${\mathrm{SrTiO}}_{3}$ by hybrid molecular beam epitaxy (hMBE). We explored the superconducting dome in this material system by tuning electron density with … [Phys. Rev. B 113, L180502] Published Mon May 11, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Sushant Padhye, Jin Yue, Shivasheesh Varshney, Bharat Jalan, David Goldhaber-Gordon, and Evgeny Mikheev

In this work, we patterned a two-dimensional electron gas (2DEG) on the surface of a SrTiO3 thin film grown homoepitaxially on SrTiO3 by hybrid molecular beam epitaxy (hMBE). We explored the superconducting dome in this material system by tuning electron density with ionic liquid gating. We found su…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L180502] Published Mon May 11, 2026
item.dc:title: [7] Superconducting dome in ionic liquid gated homoepitaxial strontium titanate thin films
item.dc:creator: Sushant Padhye, Jin Yue, Shivasheesh Varshney, Bharat Jalan, David Goldhaber-Gordon, Evgeny Mikheev
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Superfluidity and superconductivity

item.title: [7] Single-defect spectroscopy via random telegraph noise in graphene contacted ReS2−hBN heterostructures
item.description:
Last authors: Arumugum Thamizhavel, Atindra Nath Pal

Authors: Shubhrasish Mukherjee, Gaurab Samanta, Shubhadip Moulick, et al.

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Experimental spectroscopy/noise study on vdW heterostructures.

Tags: #graphene #vanderWaals #heterostructure

Abstract: Author(s): Shubhrasish Mukherjee, Gaurab Samanta, Shubhadip Moulick, Ruta Kulkarni, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, Arumugum Thamizhavel, and Atindra Nath Pal Defect spectroscopy in two-dimensional (2D) field-effect transistors (FETs) requires architectures that minimize contact and disorder-induced artifacts while preserving intrinsic carrier dynamics. We realize ${\mathrm{ReS}}_{2}\text{−}h\mathrm{BN}$ FETs with few-layer graphene (FLG) van der Waals co… [Phys. Rev. B 113, L201402] Published Mon May 11, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Shubhrasish Mukherjee, Gaurab Samanta, Shubhadip Moulick, Ruta Kulkarni, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, Arumugum Thamizhavel, and Atindra Nath Pal

Defect spectroscopy in two-dimensional (2D) field-effect transistors (FETs) requires architectures that minimize contact and disorder-induced artifacts while preserving intrinsic carrier dynamics. We realize ReS2−hBN FETs with few-layer graphene (FLG) van der Waals contacts that form nearly barrier-…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L201402] Published Mon May 11, 2026
item.dc:title: [7] Single-defect spectroscopy via random telegraph noise in graphene contacted ReS2−hBN heterostructures
item.dc:creator: Shubhrasish Mukherjee, Gaurab Samanta, Shubhadip Moulick, et al.

item.title: [8] Orbital mixing and strong Hund's coupling stabilize spin order in the van der Waals ferromagnet CrI3
item.description:
Last authors: R. Ernstorfer, T. Pincelli

Authors: A. De Vita, S. Stavrić, R. Sant, et al.

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Study of spin order in a van der Waals ferromagnet, relevant to 2D materials and magnetism.

Tags: #vanderWaals #ferromagnetism #2Dmaterials #electronicstructure

Abstract: Author(s): A. De Vita, S. Stavrić, R. Sant, N. B. Brookes, I. Vobornik, G. Panaccione, S. Picozzi, M. Wolf, L. Rettig, R. Ernstorfer, and T. Pincelli Recent years have seen a vast increase in research into van der Waals magnetic materials. In many of these systems, magnetism is introduced via light $3d$ transition metal elements, combined with chalcogenides or halogens. Despite the great technological promise in the field of spintronics, the conn… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 205120] Published Fri May 08, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): A. De Vita, S. Stavrić, R. Sant, N. B. Brookes, I. Vobornik, G. Panaccione, S. Picozzi, M. Wolf, L. Rettig, R. Ernstorfer, and T. Pincelli

Recent years have seen a vast increase in research into van der Waals magnetic materials. In many of these systems, magnetism is introduced via light 3d transition metal elements, combined with chalcogenides or halogens. Despite the great technological promise in the field of spintronics, the connec…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 205120] Published Fri May 08, 2026
item.dc:title: [8] Orbital mixing and strong Hund's coupling stabilize spin order in the van der Waals ferromagnet CrI3

item.title: [9] Room-temperature intrinsic anomalous Hall effect and robust half-metallicity in the quaternary Heusler alloy CoRuFeSi
item.description:
Last authors: V. Kanchana, Tapas Paramanik

Authors: Manikantha Panda, Sonali S Pradhan, Prabuddha Kant Mishra, et al.

Score 9/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Experimental study of intrinsic anomalous Hall effect in a Heusler alloy, a direct user interest.

Tags: #anomalousHall #ferromagnetism #Heusleralloy #transport

Abstract: Author(s): Manikantha Panda, Sonali S Pradhan, Prabuddha Kant Mishra, Alapan Bera, Rosni Roy, Soumik Mukhopadhyay, Rajib Mondal, V. Kanchana, and Tapas Paramanik We report a comprehensive study of the magnetic, magnetotransport, and Hall transport properties of the quaternary Heusler alloy CoRuFeSi. The compound has a saturation magnetization of 4.21 ${µ}_{B}$/f.u. and is classified as a soft ferromagnet at room temperature. The longitudinal resistivity exhi… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 205122] Published Fri May 08, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Manikantha Panda, Sonali S Pradhan, Prabuddha Kant Mishra, Alapan Bera, Rosni Roy, Soumik Mukhopadhyay, Rajib Mondal, V. Kanchana, and Tapas Paramanik

We report a comprehensive study of the magnetic, magnetotransport, and Hall transport properties of the quaternary Heusler alloy CoRuFeSi. The compound has a saturation magnetization of 4.21 µB/f.u. and is classified as a soft ferromagnet at room temperature. The longitudinal resistivity exhibits an…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 205122] Published Fri May 08, 2026
item.dc:title: [9] Room-temperature intrinsic anomalous Hall effect and robust half-metallicity in the quaternary Heusler alloy CoRuFeSi

item.title: [8] Physics of the spin-orbit coupling at the surface of the model topological insulators Bi2Se3 and Bi2Te3: Theory and experiments
item.description:
Last authors: F. Cilento, H. Ebert, J. Braun, and F. Parmigiani

Authors: D. Puntel, F. Sammartino, W. Bronsch, S. Peli, F. Cilento, H. Ebert, J. Braun, and F. Parmigiani

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Theory and experiments on spin-orbit coupling and surface states in classic topological insulators.

Tags: #topologicalinsulator #spin-orbitcoupling #surfacestates #experiment #theory

Abstract: Author(s): D. Puntel, F. Sammartino, W. Bronsch, S. Peli, F. Cilento, H. Ebert, J. Braun, and F. Parmigiani The dispersion of topological surface states in three-dimensional topological insulators (TIs) is commonly attributed to the atomic spin-orbit coupling (SOC) of the constituent elements. Whether the observable surface-state dispersion reflects this bare atomic value, or instead an effective SOC reno… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 205308] Published Fri May 08, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): D. Puntel, F. Sammartino, W. Bronsch, S. Peli, F. Cilento, H. Ebert, J. Braun, and F. Parmigiani

The dispersion of topological surface states in three-dimensional topological insulators (TIs) is commonly attributed to the atomic spin-orbit coupling (SOC) of the constituent elements. Whether the observable surface-state dispersion reflects this bare atomic value, or instead an effective SOC reno…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 205308] Published Fri May 08, 2026
item.dc:title: [8] Physics of the spin-orbit coupling at the surface of the model topological insulators Bi2Se3 and Bi2Te3: Theory and experiments
item.dc:creator: D. Puntel, F. Sammartino, W. Bronsch, S. Peli, F. Cilento, H. Ebert, J. Braun, and F. Parmigiani

item.title: [7] Color degeneracy of competing orders near topological defect cores in planar quadratic band touching systems
item.description:
Last authors: Bitan Roy

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Theory on competing orders and topological defects in Bernal bilayer graphene, relevant to user.

Tags: #graphene #topological #superconductivity #theory

Abstract: Author(s): Bitan Roy We study two-dimensional fermionic systems, displaying quadratic band touching in the normal state, in the presence of vortices and skyrmions of insulating and superconducting masses in the ordered phase. A prototypical example of such systems is the Bernal bilayer graphene that supports eight zero-… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 205410] Published Fri May 08, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Bitan Roy

We study two-dimensional fermionic systems, displaying quadratic band touching in the normal state, in the presence of vortices and skyrmions of insulating and superconducting masses in the ordered phase. A prototypical example of such systems is the Bernal bilayer graphene that supports eight zero-…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 205410] Published Fri May 08, 2026
item.dc:title: [7] Color degeneracy of competing orders near topological defect cores in planar quadratic band touching systems
item.dc:creator: Bitan Roy
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Surface physics, nanoscale physics, low-dimensional systems
item.prism:section: Surface physics, nanoscale physics, low-dimensional systems

item.title: [7] Enhanced superconducting diode effect in hybrid Josephson junctions
item.description:
Last authors: Enrico Rossi, Javad Shabani

Authors: Peng Yu, Han Fu, William F. Schiela, et al.

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Study of enhanced superconducting diode effect in hybrid Josephson junctions, relevant to superconductivity.

Tags: #superconductivity #Josephsonjunction #experiment

Abstract: Author(s): Peng Yu, Han Fu, William F. Schiela, William Strickland, Bassel Heiba Elfeky, S. M. Farzaneh, Jacob Issokson, Wei Pan, Enrico Rossi, and Javad Shabani Engineering the geometry of planar Josephson junctions can unlock enhanced functionalities, including an increased superconducting gap and a stronger superconducting diode effect. Here, the authors introduce a novel junction geometry featuring gate-tunable hole arrays embedded within the superconducting leads, enabling control over Andreev reflection. When a top gate depletes the 2DEG in the hole-array region, the superconducting diode effect is significantly enhanced while the overall supercurrent remains largely preserved. Theoretical analysis reveals that this enhancement originates from increased asymmetry in band-dependent transparency across the junction. [Phys. Rev. B 113, 205411] Published Fri May 08, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Peng Yu, Han Fu, William F. Schiela, William Strickland, Bassel Heiba Elfeky, S. M. Farzaneh, Jacob Issokson, Wei Pan, Enrico Rossi, and Javad Shabani

Engineering the geometry of planar Josephson junctions can unlock enhanced functionalities, including an increased superconducting gap and a stronger superconducting diode effect. Here, the authors introduce a novel junction geometry featuring gate-tunable hole arrays embedded within the superconducting leads, enabling control over Andreev reflection. When a top gate depletes the 2DEG in the hole-array region, the superconducting diode effect is significantly enhanced while the overall supercurrent remains largely preserved. Theoretical analysis reveals that this enhancement originates from increased asymmetry in band-dependent transparency across the junction.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 205411] Published Fri May 08, 2026
item.dc:title: [7] Enhanced superconducting diode effect in hybrid Josephson junctions
item.dc:creator: Peng Yu, Han Fu, William F. Schiela, et al.
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Surface physics, nanoscale physics, low-dimensional systems
item.prism:section: Surface physics, nanoscale physics, low-dimensional systems

item.title: [7] Theory of nonlinear spectroscopy of quantum magnets
item.description:
Last authors: Michael Knap, Johannes Knolle

Authors: Anubhav Srivastava, Stefan Birnkammer, GiBaik Sim, Michael Knap, Johannes Knolle

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Theory of nonlinear spectroscopy for quantum magnets, proposing experimental protocols for a real material.

Tags: #quantummagnets #spectroscopy #theory #experimentproposal

Abstract: Author(s): Anubhav Srivastava, Stefan Birnkammer, GiBaik Sim, Michael Knap, and Johannes Knolle Here, the authors demonstrate that the two-dimensional coherent spectroscopic (2DCS) response of quantum magnets is influenced not only by magnetization couplings but also by magnetoelectric and polarization couplings. The authors incorporate these couplings into a minimal model for CoNb 2 O 6 and show that new response features provide information about exotic excitations. An experimental protocol is proposed to detect the coupling between electric field and spin degrees of freedom, and the role of symmetries for the nonlinear response is investigated. [Phys. Rev. B 113, 184421] Published Thu May 07, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Anubhav Srivastava, Stefan Birnkammer, GiBaik Sim, Michael Knap, and Johannes Knolle

Here, the authors demonstrate that the two-dimensional coherent spectroscopic (2DCS) response of quantum magnets is influenced not only by magnetization couplings but also by magnetoelectric and polarization couplings. The authors incorporate these couplings into a minimal model for CoNb2O6 and show that new response features provide information about exotic excitations. An experimental protocol is proposed to detect the coupling between electric field and spin degrees of freedom, and the role of symmetries for the nonlinear response is investigated.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 184421] Published Thu May 07, 2026
item.dc:title: [7] Theory of nonlinear spectroscopy of quantum magnets
item.dc:creator: Anubhav Srivastava, Stefan Birnkammer, GiBaik Sim, Michael Knap, Johannes Knolle
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Magnetism

item.title: [8] Light-induced magnetization dynamics in the chiral magnet MnSi
item.description:
Last authors: David Mandrus, Denis Karaiskaj

Authors: Arup Barua, Sean Knapp, Jacob Gayles, et al.

Score 8/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Experimental study of light-induced magnetization dynamics in the chiral magnet MnSi.

Tags: #chiralmagnet #magnetism #TRSB #experiment #MnSi

Abstract: Author(s): Arup Barua, Sean Knapp, Jacob Gayles, Tobias Weber, Theodoros Adamantopoulos, Yuriy Mokrousov, Stephen A. McGill, Shirin Mozaffari, Matthew Cothrine, David Mandrus, and Denis Karaiskaj We perform time-resolved magneto-optical Kerr effect measurements and record the magnetization dynamics for external magnetic fields along various symmetry axes of the chiral magnet MnSi. The different orientations of the magnetic field result in very different magnetization dynamics. For magnetic f… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 184424] Published Thu May 07, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Arup Barua, Sean Knapp, Jacob Gayles, Tobias Weber, Theodoros Adamantopoulos, Yuriy Mokrousov, Stephen A. McGill, Shirin Mozaffari, Matthew Cothrine, David Mandrus, and Denis Karaiskaj

We perform time-resolved magneto-optical Kerr effect measurements and record the magnetization dynamics for external magnetic fields along various symmetry axes of the chiral magnet MnSi. The different orientations of the magnetic field result in very different magnetization dynamics. For magnetic f…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 184424] Published Thu May 07, 2026
item.dc:title: [8] Light-induced magnetization dynamics in the chiral magnet MnSi
item.dc:creator: Arup Barua, Sean Knapp, Jacob Gayles, et al.
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Magnetism

item.title: [7] Statistical characterization of the spin Hall magnetoresistance in YIG/Pt heterostructures
item.description:
Last authors: Michaela Lammel, Sebastian T. B. Goennenwein

Authors: Denise Reustlen, Sebastian Sailler, Davina U. Schmidt, et al.

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Experimental study of spin Hall magnetoresistance in YIG/Pt heterostructures, relevant to spintronics.

Tags: #heterostructure #spintronics #ferromagnetism #experiment

Abstract: Author(s): Denise Reustlen, Sebastian Sailler, Davina U. Schmidt, Rony Werner, Richard Schlitz, Michaela Lammel, and Sebastian T. B. Goennenwein The spin Hall magnetoresistance (SMR) is a sensitive probe for interfacial spin currents in heavy metal/ferromagnet bilayers. In a statistical experimental approach, the authors compare here the SMR of hundreds of Hall bars on a single sample, observing SMR amplitude variations by up to 10%. Moreover, the average SMR on several nominally identical samples differs by up to 35%. Spatial variations of the spin mixing conductance should thus be carefully considered when analysing variations of the SMR amplitude. [Phys. Rev. B 113, 184427] Published Thu May 07, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Denise Reustlen, Sebastian Sailler, Davina U. Schmidt, Rony Werner, Richard Schlitz, Michaela Lammel, and Sebastian T. B. Goennenwein

The spin Hall magnetoresistance (SMR) is a sensitive probe for interfacial spin currents in heavy metal/ferromagnet bilayers. In a statistical experimental approach, the authors compare here the SMR of hundreds of Hall bars on a single sample, observing SMR amplitude variations by up to 10%. Moreover, the average SMR on several nominally identical samples differs by up to 35%. Spatial variations of the spin mixing conductance should thus be carefully considered when analysing variations of the SMR amplitude.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 184427] Published Thu May 07, 2026
item.dc:title: [7] Statistical characterization of the spin Hall magnetoresistance in YIG/Pt heterostructures
item.dc:creator: Denise Reustlen, Sebastian Sailler, Davina U. Schmidt, et al.
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Magnetism

item.title: [7] Microscopic magnetism of A(TiO)Cu4(PO4)4 (A = Ba, Pb, Sr): 31P and 63,65Cu NMR study
item.description:
Last authors: Kenta Kimura, Raivo Stern

Authors: Riho Rästa, Ivo Heinmaa, Joosep Link, et al.

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Experimental NMR study of microscopic magnetism in chiral square-cupola antiferromagnets.

Tags: #magnetism #NMR #antiferromagnet #experiment

Abstract: Author(s): Riho Rästa, Ivo Heinmaa, Joosep Link, Yusuke Kousaka, Tsuyoshi Kimura, Yoshihiko Ihara, Kenta Kimura, and Raivo Stern We report a comprehensive NMR study of the chiral square-cupola antiferromagnet $\mathrm{Pb}(\mathrm{TiO}){\mathrm{Cu}}_{4}{({\mathrm{PO}}_{4})}_{4}$ and compare its microscopic hyperfine and local-field parameters with the Ba/Sr analogs in the $A(\mathrm{TiO}){\mathrm{Cu}}_{4}{({\mathrm{PO}}_{4})}_… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 184428] Published Thu May 07, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Riho Rästa, Ivo Heinmaa, Joosep Link, Yusuke Kousaka, Tsuyoshi Kimura, Yoshihiko Ihara, Kenta Kimura, and Raivo Stern

We report a comprehensive NMR study of the chiral square-cupola antiferromagnet Pb(TiO)Cu4(PO4)4 and compare its microscopic hyperfine and local-field parameters with the Ba/Sr analogs in the A(TiO)Cu4(PO4)4 family. Above TN≃6.7K, the P31 Knight shift tracks the bulk susceptibility and yields nearly…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 184428] Published Thu May 07, 2026
item.dc:title: [7] Microscopic magnetism of A(TiO)Cu4(PO4)4 (A = Ba, Pb, Sr): 31P and 63,65Cu NMR study
item.dc:creator: Riho Rästa, Ivo Heinmaa, Joosep Link, et al.
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited

Different from the reported family members with light $R$ elements exhibiting non… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 184429] Published Thu May 07, 2026

Rare earth-transition metal compounds RMn2Ge2 (R=rare earth element) exhibit rich magnetic structures and intriguing physical phenomena, which have attracted sustained research interest. Different from the reported family members with light R elements exhibiting noncoplanar magnetic structures, GdMn…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 184429] Published Thu May 07, 2026

Tags: #kagome #antiferromagnet

Abstract: Author(s): Chenglin Shang, Daye Xu, Bingxian Shi, Xuejuan Gui, Zhongcen Sun, Juanjuan Liu, Jinchen Wang, Hongxia Zhang, Hongliang Wang, Lijie Hao, and Peng Cheng CrRhAs was theoretically proposed to be a kagome metal with unusual magnetic ground states; however, little is known about its magnetic structure and physical properties experimentally. Here, we present an experimental investigation of CrRhAs with ZrNiAl-type structure and a distorted Cr kagome latt… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 184430] Published Thu May 07, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Chenglin Shang, Daye Xu, Bingxian Shi, Xuejuan Gui, Zhongcen Sun, Juanjuan Liu, Jinchen Wang, Hongxia Zhang, Hongliang Wang, Lijie Hao, and Peng Cheng

CrRhAs was theoretically proposed to be a kagome metal with unusual magnetic ground states; however, little is known about its magnetic structure and physical properties experimentally. Here, we present an experimental investigation of CrRhAs with ZrNiAl-type structure and a distorted Cr kagome latt…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 184430] Published Thu May 07, 2026

Wu, and Aimei Zhang Surface magnetoelectric (ME) coupling is conventionally attributed to strong spin-orbit coupling (SOC) or interface-induced polarization and is therefore considered strongly limited in centrosymmetric antiferromagnets. Here we show, theoretically, that rutile-type transition-metal fluorides ${M\math… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 184431] Published Thu May 07, 2026

Surface magnetoelectric (ME) coupling is conventionally attributed to strong spin-orbit coupling (SOC) or interface-induced polarization and is therefore considered strongly limited in centrosymmetric antiferromagnets. Here we show, theoretically, that rutile-type transition-metal fluorides MF2 (M=V…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 184431] Published Thu May 07, 2026

Xavier Symmetries strongly influence transport properties of quantum many-body systems and can lead to deviations from the generic case of diffusion. In this work, we study the impact of time-reversal symmetry breaking on the transport and its universal aspects in integrable chiral spin ladders. We observe… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 184305] Published Wed May 06, 2026

Symmetries strongly influence transport properties of quantum many-body systems and can lead to deviations from the generic case of diffusion. In this work, we study the impact of time-reversal symmetry breaking on the transport and its universal aspects in integrable chiral spin ladders. We observe…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 184305] Published Wed May 06, 2026

Wójcik ${\mathrm{MnBi}}_{2}{\mathrm{Te}}_{4}$ (MBT) is the first intrinsic magnetic topological insulator, combining a topologically protected surface metallic state and intrinsic magnetic order. A structural compatibility with the nonmagnetic ${\mathrm{Bi}}_{2}{\mathrm{Te}}_{3}$ (BT) parent compound gives… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 184420] Published Wed May 06, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): R. Kalvig, E. Jedryka, A. Lynnyk, P. Skupiński, K. Grasza, and M. Wójcik

MnBi2Te4 (MBT) is the first intrinsic magnetic topological insulator, combining a topologically protected surface metallic state and intrinsic magnetic order. A structural compatibility with the nonmagnetic Bi2Te3 (BT) parent compound gives a possibility to create MBT/BT heterostructures and manipul…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 184420] Published Wed May 06, 2026

Abstract: Author(s): Xiaofan Shen, Ke Ji, Xiaomei Lu, Junting Zhang, and Milorad V. Milošević Magnetoelectric coupling in multiferroics provides a low-energy means of manipulating magnetic spin textures, including topological ones such as skyrmions, using an electric field instead of a current. However, existing demonstrations were realized in multiferroic heterostructures, relying on ferroe… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 184422] Published Wed May 06, 2026

However, existing demonstrations were realized in multiferroic heterostructures, relying on ferroe…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 184422] Published Wed May 06, 2026

Spin dynamics equations are accurately solved to obtain magnon energies and corresponding density fluctuations. We demonstrate that magnon wave function… [Phys. Rev. B 113, 184423] Published Wed May 06, 2026
